title: Tau Intermediate Logical Representation (ILR) Schema
description: Defines the structure for Tau ILR, representing programs in a standardized, machine-readable format.

CoreDataType.description: Enumerates fundamental data types supported by the ILR.

TemporalQualifier.description: Specifies a temporal offset for stream or time-dependent variable access.

offset.description: Integer offset: 0 for current time [t], -1 for [t-1], etc.

NumericConstant.description: Represents a literal numeric value (integer or floating-point).

width.description: Optional width of the bitvector in bits.

BitstringConstant.description: Represents a constant bitstring value (e.g., '1010xz01'). Allows 'x' (don't care) and 'z' (high-impedance).

value.description: The bitstring value as a string. Should only contain '0', '1', 'x', 'z'.

length.description: The number of characters in the bitstring value.

VariableReference.description: Represents a reference to a declared variable, stream, or entity.

name.description: The unique name/identifier of the variable or stream.

temporal_qualifier.description: Optional temporal qualifier for time-dependent variable access.

LogicalExpression.description: Represents a logical operation (AND, OR, XOR, NOT).

operands.description: An array of one or more expressions (for NOT) or two or more expressions (for AND, OR, XOR).

ComparisonExpression.description: Represents a comparison between two expressions.

left_operand.description: The left operand of the comparison.

right_operand.description: The right operand of the comparison.

left_operand.description: The left operand of the arithmetic operation.

right_operand.description: The right operand of the arithmetic operation.

UnaryArithmeticExpression.description: Represents a unary arithmetic operation (e.g., negation).

operand.description: The operand of the unary arithmetic operation.

FunctionParameter.description: Represents a parameter for a function call, can be positional (just an expression) or named.

parameter_name.description: Name of the parameter for named arguments.

function_name.description: Name of the function to call. This could also be an expression that evaluates to a function reference in more advanced scenarios, but for now, a string name is assumed.

arguments.description: An array of arguments for the function call.

value_if_true.description: The expression to evaluate if the condition is true.

value_if_false.description: The expression to evaluate if the condition is false.

TypeConversionExpression.description: Represents an explicit type conversion of an expression.

target_type.description: The data type to convert the expression to.

width.description: Optional. For conversions to BITVECTOR, specifies the target width in bits.

radix.description: Optional. For STRING to NUMBER or BITVECTOR, specifies the radix (e.g., 2, 10, 16).

PaddingBitType.description: Type of bit to use for padding.

TruncationSide.description: Side from which to truncate bits.

BitExtractionExpression.description: Extracts a range of bits from a bitvector or bitstring.

msb_index.description: Most significant bit index (inclusive). Indexing from 0 (LSB).

lsb_index.description: Least significant bit index (inclusive). Indexing from 0 (LSB).

BitConcatenationExpression.description: Concatenates two or more bitvector or bitstring expressions.

operands.description: An array of two or more bitvector/bitstring expressions to concatenate.
An array of one (for NOT) or two (for AND, OR, etc.) bitvector/bitstring expressions.

BitPaddingExpression.description: Pads a bitvector or bitstring to a target width.

target_width.description: The desired width after padding. Must be >= original width.

padding_bit.description: The bit type to use for padding.

padding_side.description: The side to which padding bits are added.

BitTruncationExpression.description: Truncates a bitvector or bitstring to a target width.

target_width.description: The desired width after truncation. Must be <= original width.

truncation_from.description: The side from which bits are removed.

ArrayLiteral.description: Represents an array literal expression, e.g., [1, 2, 3].

elements.description: An array of expressions representing the elements of the literal.

AddressOfExpression.description: Represents taking the address of a variable (e.g., &var).

operand.description: The LValue expression whose address is being taken.
The expression that evaluates to a pointer.

AnyExpressionNode.description: Represents any valid ILR expression node.

DirectionEnum.description: Specifies the direction of a variable or parameter, typically for function arguments or module ports.

VariableDeclaration.description: Declares a variable, constant, stream, or parameter.

name.description: The unique name/identifier of the variable.

data_type.description: The data type of the variable.

width.description: Optional. For BITVECTOR type, specifies the width in bits.

is_constant.description: Optional. If true, the variable is a constant and must have an initial value. Defaults to false.

is_stream.description: Optional. If true, the variable represents a data stream. Defaults to false.

initial_value.description: Optional. The initial value of the variable. Required if is_constant is true.

direction.description: Optional. Specifies the direction, primarily for function parameters or module ports.

FunctionDeclaration.description: Declares a function, its parameters, return type, and optionally its body.

name.description: The unique name/identifier of the function.

parameters.description: An array of variable declarations representing the function parameters.

return_type.description: The data type of the value returned by the function. Use 'VOID' if the function does not return a value.

return_width.description: Optional. If return_type is BITVECTOR, specifies the width in bits.

body.description: Optional. An array of statements forming the function's body.

AnyDeclarationNode.description: Represents any valid ILR declaration node (e.g., variable, function).

AssignmentStatement.description: Assigns a value from an expression to a variable or other writable target.

target.description: The variable or writable reference to assign to.

expression.description: The expression whose value is assigned.

ReturnStatement.description: Returns a value from a function. If the function's return type is VOID, the expression may be omitted.

expression.description: Optional. The expression to return. Required if function return type is not VOID.

ProcedureCallStatement.description: Represents a call to a procedure (a function called for its side effects). Structurally similar to FunctionCall.

procedure_name.description: Name of the procedure to call.

arguments.description: An array of argument expressions for the procedure call.

ElseIfClause.description: An else-if clause for an IfStatement.

condition.description: The condition for this else-if branch.

then_block.description: An array of statements to execute if this else-if condition is true.
An array of statements to execute if the primary condition is true.

else_if_clauses.description: Optional. An array of else-if clauses.

else_block.description: Optional. An array of statements to execute if all preceding conditions are false.

LoopTypeEnum.description: Enumerates different types of loop constructs.

initialization.description: Optional. Statement(s) or declaration for loop initialization.

increment.description: Optional. Expression or statement executed after each iteration.

body.description: An array of statements forming the loop's body.
An array of statements forming the loop's body.
An array of statements forming the loop's body.

condition.description: Loop continuation condition, evaluated after the body.

ForEachLoopStatement.description: Represents a for-each loop, iterating over an iterable expression.

iterator_variable.description: Declaration of the variable that takes on the value of each item in the iterable.

iterable_expression.description: The expression that evaluates to an iterable collection or range.

body.description: An array of statements forming the loop's body.

LoopControlStatement.description: Represents a loop control statement like BREAK or CONTINUE.

AssertionStatement.description: Asserts that a given condition is true. If not, it implies an error or failure.

message.description: Optional. A message to associate with the assertion, e.g., for logging on failure.

WaitStatement.description: Pauses execution until a condition is met or a timeout occurs.

condition.description: The condition to wait for. Execution resumes when this becomes true.

timeout_value.description: Optional. An expression evaluating to a duration or time value for timeout.

timeout_block.description: Optional. An array of statements to execute if the wait times out.

ExpressionStatement.description: A statement that consists of an expression evaluated for its side effects. The expression's value is discarded.

EmptyStatement.description: A statement that performs no operation (no-op).

CaseClause.description: Represents a case within a SwitchStatement.

values.description: An array of literal values to match against the switch discriminant. The case is selected if any of these values match.

block.description: An array of statements to execute if this case matches.

fallthrough.description: Optional. If true, execution continues to the next case's block after this block completes. Defaults to false.

SwitchStatement.description: Represents a switch statement for multi-way branching.

discriminant.description: The expression whose value is switched upon.

default_block.description: Optional. An array of statements to execute if no case matches.

CatchClause.description: Represents a catch block within a TryCatchFinallyStatement.

exception_variable.description: Variable declaration for the caught exception. Its type may indicate the type of exception handled.

block.description: An array of statements to execute if an exception is caught by this clause.

TryCatchFinallyStatement.description: Represents a try-catch-finally block for structured exception handling.

try_block.description: An array of statements forming the try block.

catch_clauses.description: An array of catch clauses to handle exceptions.

finally_block.description: Optional. An array of statements forming the finally block, executed after try/catch.

ThrowStatement.description: Represents a statement that throws an exception.

expression.description: The expression evaluating to the exception object to be thrown.

AnnotationNode.description: Represents a non-operational annotation, such as a comment or pragma.

content.description: The textual content of the annotation.

annotation_type.description: Optional. Categorizes the annotation (e.g., COMMENT, PRAGMA).

AnyStatementNode.description: Represents any valid ILR statement node.

StructField.description: Defines a field within a StructDefinition.

data_type.description: Data type of the struct field.

StructDefinition.description: Defines a structure (record) type, a composite type with named fields.

fields.description: An array of fields that compose the struct.

description.description: Optional description for the struct type.

EnumMember.description: Defines a member (named constant) of an EnumDefinition.

value.description: Optional. Explicit integer value for the enum member. If omitted, values may be auto-assigned.

description.description: Optional description for the enum member.

EnumDefinition.description: Defines an enumeration type, a set of named constants.

members.description: An array of members (named constants) in the enum.

base_type.description: Optional. The underlying core data type for the enum (e.g., INTEGER). Defaults to INTEGER if not specified.

description.description: Optional description for the enum type.

TypeAliasDefinition.description: Defines an alias (alternative name) for an existing data type.

name.description: The new alias name for the type.

description.description: Optional description for the type alias.

ImportedSymbol.description: Specifies a symbol to be imported and its optional alias.

symbol_name.description: The name of the symbol to import.

alias.description: Optional alias for the imported symbol.

ImportDeclaration.description: Declares an import of symbols from another module/program_unit.

module_name.description: Name or path of the module to import from.

imported_symbols.description: Optional. Specific symbols to import. If absent, may imply importing all exported symbols.

ExportedSymbol.description: Specifies a symbol to be exported and its optional alias.

symbol_name.description: The name of the symbol in the current unit to export.

alias.description: Optional. The name under which the symbol is exported.

ExportDeclaration.description: Declares symbols from the current unit to be made available for import by other units.

DataType.description: Represents a data type, which can be a core type or a user-defined type.

core_type.description: The specific core data type, if not a user-defined type.

type_name.description: Name of the type. For core types, this is redundant but can be descriptive (e.g., 'int32', 'string'). For USER_DEFINED core_type, this holds the name of the user-defined type (e.g., 'MyStruct', 'StatusEnum').

width_bits.description: Optional. For BITVECTOR core_type, specifies the width in bits.

element_type.description: Required if core_type is ARRAY. Specifies the data type of array elements.

dimensions.description: Optional. If core_type is ARRAY, specifies the size of each dimension.

referenced_type.description: Required if core_type is POINTER. Specifies the data type the pointer refers to.

ProgramUnit.description: Represents the root of a Tau ILR program or module, containing top-level declarations.

name.description: Optional. An identifier or name for this program unit (e.g., module name).

imports.description: Optional. An array of import declarations.

exports.description: Optional. An array of export declarations.

declarations.description: An array of top-level declarations (variables, functions, types, etc.).

entry_point_function.description: Optional. The name of a function defined in 'declarations' that serves as the program's entry point.

ilr_version.description: Version of the ILR schema this document conforms to.

unit.description: The main program unit containing all top-level declarations and structure.
